title: cyathea sangirensis
intro: Cyathea sangirensis, also known as the Sangire fern, is a species of tree fern that belongs to the family Cyatheaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cyathea sangirensis include treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as an antioxidant and to improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
